- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- commit: Stage and commit changes with a conventional commit message
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- triage: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
- prototype: Build a throwaway prototype to flush out a design before committing to it. Routes between two branches — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route. Use when the user wants to prototype, sanity-check a data model or state machine, mock up a UI, explore design options, or says "prototype this", "let me play with it", "try a few designs".
- diagnose: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-skill: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- to-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

import tailwindcss from "@tailwindcss/vite"

export default defineNuxtConfig({
  compatibilityDate: "2025-01-01",
  future: { compatibilityVersion: 4 },
  devtools: { enabled: true },
  typescript: { strict: true },
  nitro: { preset: "node-server" },
  css: ["~/assets/css/tailwind.css"],
  vite: { plugins: [tailwindcss()] },
  components: [{ path: "~/components", pathPrefix: false }],
  modules: ["@nuxtjs/i18n", "@nuxt/fonts", "@nuxt/eslint", "@pinia/nuxt"],
  eslint: { config: { stylistic: false } },
  fonts: {
    families: [
      { name: "Inter", provider: "google" },
      { name: "Fraunces", provider: "google" },
      { name: "JetBrains Mono", provider: "google" },
    ],
  },
  i18n: {
    strategy: "prefix",
    defaultLocale: "en",
    locales: [
      { code: "en", language: "en-US", file: "en.json" },
      { code: "zh", language: "zh-CN", file: "zh.json" },
    ],
    detectBrowserLanguage: { useCookie: true, cookieKey: "i18n_redirected", redirectOn: "root" },
  },
})

<script setup lang="ts">
definePageMeta({ middleware: ["require-auth", "require-onboard"] })

const { t } = useI18n()
const router = useRouter()
const localePath = useLocalePath()

const form = reactive({
  slug: "",
  name: "",
  version: "1.0.0",
  category: "skills" as "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
  scope: "personal" as "personal" | "org" | "enterprise",
  summary: "",
})

const fileInput = useTemplateRef<HTMLInputElement>("fileInput")
const selectedFile = ref<File | null>(null)
const error = ref<string | null>(null)
const submitting = ref(false)
const stage = ref<"idle" | "creating" | "signing" | "uploading" | "attaching" | "submitting">("idle")

async function sha256Hex(buf: ArrayBuffer): Promise<string> {
  const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest("SHA-256", buf)
  return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hash))
    .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, "0"))
    .join("")
}

async function handleSubmit() {
  if (!selectedFile.value) {
    error.value = t("publish.errors.bundleRequired")
    return
  }
  error.value = null
  submitting.value = true

  try {
    stage.value = "creating"
    const draft = await $fetch<{ ok: true; extensionId: string; versionId: string }>(
      "/api/internal/publish/create-draft",
      {
        method: "POST",
        body: {
          slug: form.slug,
          name: form.name,
          version: form.version,
          category: form.category,
          scope: form.scope,
          summary: form.summary,
          tagIds: [],
          permissions: {},
          iconColor: "indigo",
          sourceMethod: "zip",
        },
      },
    )

    stage.value = "signing"
    const file = selectedFile.value!
    const buf = await file.arrayBuffer()
    const checksum = await sha256Hex(buf)
    const signed = await $fetch<{ url: string; key: string }>(
      "/api/upload/sign",
      {
        method: "POST",
        body: { slug: form.slug, version: form.version, contentType: "application/zip" },
      },
    )

    stage.value = "uploading"
    const putRes = await fetch(signed.url, {
      method: "PUT",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/zip" },
      body: buf,
    })
    if (!putRes.ok) throw new Error(`upload failed: ${putRes.status}`)

    stage.value = "attaching"
    await $fetch("/api/internal/publish/attach-file", {
      method: "POST",
      body: {
        versionId: draft.versionId,
        r2Key: signed.key,
        size: file.size,
        checksumSha256: checksum,
      },
    })

    stage.value = "submitting"
    await $fetch("/api/internal/publish/submit", {
      method: "POST",
      body: { versionId: draft.versionId },
    })

    await router.push(localePath("/publish"))
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("publish failed", err)
    error.value = (err as { data?: { statusMessage?: string } })?.data?.statusMessage
      ?? t("publish.errors.generic")

content.content:
- **Sign-up policy**: open. Email + password for v1; SSO/SAML deferred.
- **MVP roles** (on `memberships.role`): `viewer`, `publisher`, `admin`.
- **Onboarding step** at `/[locale]/onboard` after first sign-up. Enforced by `require-onboard` middleware on the home, browse, and publish routes.
--
The `shared/extensions/state.ts` module owns every transition of `extensionVersions.status`, `files.scanStatus`, and `extensions.visibility`. Ported verbatim from the original.

---
--
| **11. Background jobs** | 1d | Inngest client + `scan-bundle` + `reindex-search` functions, webhook route at `server/api/inngest.post.ts`, status polling on publisher dashboard. Port `shared/extensions/state.ts`. |
| **12. Search FTS** | 0.5d | Apply `0002_fts_search_vector.sql` to dev DB, trgm indexes, top-bar search wired through `useFilters`. |
| **13. Polish** | 1.5d | Empty states, `error.vue` route-level boundaries, loading skeletons via `<Suspense>` + skeleton components, OG images via `@nuxtjs/og-image`, a11y pass, dark-mode polish across all surfaces. |
| **14. Deploy** | 1d | Node preset (`nitro: { preset: 'node-server' }`), env template, deploy runbook in `docs/deploy.md`, smoke test the CLI against the new URL (with `cli/src/config-store.ts` pointed at it). |

--
- **Visual regression** (Playwright screenshots): defer to P13 polish; needs a stable design first.
- **Storybook / Histoire** for component documentation: defer; not load-bearing for a v1 codebase.
- **External coverage trend service** (Codecov, Coveralls): the local-first deploy target rules out per-PR third-party uploads as a hard dependency. CI artifact is sufficient.
--
The `/api/v1` contract is the only thing in this repo that can break external consumers (the CLI). If you change a v1 endpoint's response shape, request shape, or status semantics, mark the commit with both `!` and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer:

```
--
4. Never bypass hooks (`--no-verify`). If a hook fails, investigate and fix the underlying issue rather than skipping.
5. Each phase ends with an explicit human checkpoint; the agent does not auto-start the next phase.
6. When asked to "fix a previous commit," create a new corrective commit — do not amend or rebase.
--
| `next/image` | `@nuxt/image` module (deferred to P13 polish; not P0) |
| `app/.../error.tsx` route boundary | `app/pages/.../[slug].vue` neighbour `error.vue`, or wrap pages in `<NuxtErrorBoundary>` |
| `notFound()` | `throw createError({ statusCode: 404, statusMessage: '...' })` |
| `cookies().get('theme')` in RSC | `useCookie('theme')` (works in both SSR and client) |
| `redirect('/en')` from server component | `await navigateTo('/en')` from setup, or `sendRedirect(event, '/en')` from a server endpoint |

content.text:
I have a clear picture. Let me give you a concise summary and a proposed next-step plan.

## Confirmed gaps in the Nuxt rewrite

**Routes declared P0 in `docs/plan.md` but not implemented:**

1. **`/profile`** (plan.md:194) — the entire "My Workspace" surface. Next.js has `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` + 11 components (profile-hero, profile-settings-form, section-{activity,drafts,installed,published,saved,rail}, settings-tab-link). Nuxt has none. `shared/validators/profile.ts` also wasn't ported.
2. **`/publish/[id]/edit`** (plan.md:192) — resume/edit existing drafts. Missing entirely. Once a draft exists, there's no UI to touch it.
3. **4-step publish wizard with live preview** (plan.md:294, §10). The plan specifies `<UploadWizard>` with `Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review` and a sticky live-preview pane. Reality: `pages/publish/new.vue` is a 202-line single-form upload (vs. the 439-line `wizard/index.tsx` plus 4 step files + `live-preview.tsx` in the original).

**Components self-marked "deferred" in the code:**

4. `FilterBar.vue` ships with a comment: *"DeptPicker, TagDrawer, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker are deferred to a follow-up commit."* That breaks locked decision #3 (Mode B drawer-style multi-row filters). Today you only have ScopePills + FilterChips + SortSelect.

**Missing infrastructure:**

5. **`cli/` directory**. Plan §1 has it living here; in reality it still only exists under `../agentcenter/cli/`. Since you're abandoning the old repo, this needs to move (CLAUDE.md item #8 says it's unchanged — only the base URL flips, so it's a copy + URL bump).
6. **`components/ui/`** shadcn-vue primitives. Empty. Reka UI exists in node_modules per `package.json` but no Button/Popover/Select wrappers were ported.
7. **Detail-page sub-components**: ExtTabs (README/Manifest/Permissions tabs), ExtHero, ExtAboutCard, ExtRelatedList, ShareButton, SaveButton, InstallCommand. The Nuxt detail page is 158 lines and inlines a stripped subset (vs 386 in Next.js + 7 components).

**Smaller items:**

8. **OG images** — plan §13 calls for `@nuxtjs/og-image`; not in `nuxt.config.ts`. Next.js had `app/opengraph-image.tsx` + `extensions/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx`.
9. **Loading skeletons** — `wizard-skeleton`, `dashboard-skeleton` not ported; plan §13 wants `<Suspense>` + skeletons.
10. **Playwright e2e coverage** — Nuxt has 1 spec (`theme-no-flash`). Next.js had `browse`, `detail`, `navigation` specs. Golden paths are uncovered.

**Things that look correctly carried over:**

- DB schema (5 files match), Drizzle migrations, FTS migration
- Public `/api/v1/*` contract (extensions index/slug/bundle, installs, auth/device/code+poll)
- Internal publish endpoints (create-draft / attach-file / submit / discard / my-extensions)
- Inngest jobs (scan-bundle, reindex-search), auth handler, upload signing
- `shared/` (validators except profile, tags, taxonomy, theme, search, types, data)
- Composables (useFilters, useAuth, useTheme), i18n locale files, layouts/default.vue

## Suggested next steps

I'd tackle these roughly in this order — they map to the work the plan said was P0:

1. **Port `cli/`** into this repo (one commit, mechanical). Cheapest, smallest risk, unblocks "old repo deletable".
2. **Build out `components/ui/`** with the Reka UI primitives the wizard and filter drawer need (Button, Dialog, Popover, Select, Tabs, Drawer). Foundational for #3–#5.
3. **Filter drawer pickers** (DeptPicker, TagDrawer, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker) — restores locked decision #3 and uses #2.
4. **4-step publish wizard + `publish/[id]/edit`** — biggest piece; depends on #2.
5. **Profile page + components + `validators/profile.ts`** — last big surface.
6. **P13-style polish round 2**: detail-page tabs/hero/related, OG images, loading skeletons, the missing Playwright specs.

If you'd like, I can start with #1 (port the CLI) since it's quick and unblocks deleting the old repo — or jump straight to #2/#3, which give the most user-visible payoff. Which way do you want to go?

Here's how I'd phase it, following the project's existing conventions (one PR per phase, multiple commits per phase, conventional commits with phase scope, human checkpoint at the end of each phase).

## Plan — round 2 (P14–P20)

The existing plan.md uses P0–P13 and treats those as complete. I'd continue the numbering rather than rewrite history.

### P14 — CLI port (~0.5d, 3–5 commits)
Smallest piece, mechanical, unblocks deleting `../agentcenter/`.
- `chore(p14-cli): copy cli/ from original repo into this workspace`
- `chore(p14-cli): point CLI default base URL at the new deployment`
- `docs(p14-cli): note CLI now lives in this repo`
- Smoke test against a local Nitro server.

### P15 — UI primitives (~1d, ~8 commits)
Foundation for P16/P17/P18. Use shadcn-vue's CLI (`npx shadcn-vue add …`) so we don't hand-write Reka wrappers.
- `chore(p15-ui): add components.json + shadcn-vue config`
- One commit per primitive: Button, Input, Label, Textarea, Select, Popover, Dialog, Sheet (drawer), Tabs, Checkbox, Skeleton.
- Add an interactive smoke route or Storybook-style index if useful (optional).

### P16 — Filter drawer pickers (~1d, ~6 commits)
Restores locked decision #3 (Mode B drawer multi-row).
- `feat(p16-filters): add facets endpoint at server/api/internal/facets.get.ts` (if not already present)
- `feat(p16-filters): add DeptPicker` + tests
- `feat(p16-filters): add TagDrawer` + tests
- `feat(p16-filters): add CreatorPicker`
- `feat(p16-filters): add PublisherPicker`
- `feat(p16-filters): wire pickers into FilterBar, remove "deferred" comment`

### P17 — Publish wizard + `[id]/edit` (~2d, ~10 commits)
Biggest piece. Port the React structure but rebuild forms in Vue with vee-validate + Zod (Zod schemas already in `shared/validators/manifest.ts`).
- `feat(p17-wizard): port wizard/shared.ts state types + composable`
- `feat(p17-wizard): add WizardLivePreview` + tests
- `feat(p17-wizard): add Step 1 Basics` + tests
- `feat(p17-wizard): add Step 2 Bundle (file pick, sha256, signed PUT)`
- `feat(p17-wizard): add Step 3 Listing` + tests
- `feat(p17-wizard): add Step 4 Review`
- `feat(p17-wizard): assemble UploadWizard root, rewrite pages/publish/new.vue`
- `feat(p17-wizard): add pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue (resume flow)`
- `feat(p17-wizard): port DiscardButton + wizard-skeleton + dashboard-skeleton`
- Server-side: add any missing `internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` endpoint the edit flow needs.

### P18 — Profile page (~2d, ~12 commits)
- `chore(p18-profile): port shared/validators/profile.ts`
- `feat(p18-profile): add server/utils/queries/profile.ts` (port from `lib/db/queries/profile.ts`)
- `feat(p18-profile): add internal endpoints for profile sections + settings update`
- One commit each for: ProfileHero, ProfileSettingsForm, SectionRail, SettingsTabLink, ComingSoon, SectionActivity, SectionDrafts, SectionInstalled, SectionPublished, SectionSaved
- `feat(p18-profile): assemble pages/profile.vue`
- Tests alongside each interactive component (>=80% per CLAUDE.md threshold).

### P19 — Detail-page sub-components (~1d, ~7 commits)
Break the 158-line monolith into the same shape as the original.
- `refactor(p19-detail): extract ExtHero from pages/extensions/[slug].vue`
- `feat(p19-detail): add ExtAboutCard side panel`
- `feat(p19-detail): add ExtTabs (README / Manifest / Permissions)` + tests
- `feat(p19-detail): add ExtRelatedList`
- `feat(p19-detail): add InstallCommand` + tests
- `feat(p19-detail): add ShareButton` + tests
- `feat(p19-detail): add SaveButton` + tests

### P20 — Polish round 2 (~1d, ~6 commits)
- `chore(p20-polish): add @nuxtjs/og-image module`
- `feat(p20-polish): root OG image template`
- `feat(p20-polish): extension detail OG image`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright browse.spec.ts`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright detail.spec.ts`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright navigation.spec.ts`

## A few things I'd flag before we start

1. **Wizard rebuild, not 1:1 port.** The React wizard uses React Hook Form + RSC; the Vue version will use vee-validate + Zod + `$fetch`. Same UX, different plumbing — don't try to match line-for-line.
2. **`shared/**` coverage gate.** New profile queries and wizard pure logic land in `shared/`, which requires ≥95% line coverage. Plan tests in the same commit as the code.
3. **`docs/plan.md` update.** Per CLAUDE.md, when a binding decision changes (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo, not "carried over"), update plan.md in the same commit.
4. **One PR per phase.** P17 and P18 are the big ones; if they balloon past ~15 commits we should consider splitting (e.g. P17a wizard skeleton + Basics, P17b remaining steps + edit page).
5. **Checkpoint at the end of each phase** — I'll pause for your go-ahead before opening the next branch.

Want me to start with **P14** (port the CLI, lowest risk, smallest blast radius) on a `feat/p14-cli` branch? Or jump straight to **P15** (UI primitives) since everything else depends on it?

message.content: one PR for a phase seems too much, and multiple commits seems too much too. I am thing one PR for total, and one or several commits for each phase. what do you think?

content.text:
That works — one PR with phase-sized commits keeps things manageable without the per-step commit ceremony. Tradeoffs worth flagging:

- **Departs from CLAUDE.md** ("commit on each step"). It's your call; the PR description should mention the deviation so future-you doesn't trip on it.
- **One big PR** (~10–15 commits, probably 3–5k LOC) is harder to review but easier to ship. The phase commits still give a bisect-friendly history on `main` (you don't squash on merge per project policy).
- **PR validate gate** runs once at the end. Per-commit typecheck still required.
- **Per-phase human checkpoint** still applies (pause before starting the next phase), per CLAUDE.md.

Let me write the plan file.

# Finish the Nuxt rewrite — P14–P20 in one PR

## Context

This repo (`agentcenter-nuxt/`) is the Nuxt 4 rewrite of `../agentcenter/`. Phases P0–P13 of `docs/plan.md` shipped, but several routes and components that the plan marked **P0** were skipped or scoped down. The user is abandoning the original repo, so the remaining gaps must land here before the old tree can be deleted.

Confirmed gaps (verified against both trees on 2026-05-13):

- **`cli/` directory missing.** Plan §1 has it living here; today it only exists in `../agentcenter/cli/`. CLAUDE.md item #8 says it carries over unchanged — only the base URL flips.
- **`/profile` route + 11 components missing.** Plan.md:194 lists this as P0. `shared/validators/profile.ts` was not ported either.
- **`/publish/[id]/edit` route missing.** Plan.md:192. Drafts created via `publish/new` cannot be resumed.
- **4-step publish wizard with live preview missing.** Plan §10 (plan.md:294) specifies `Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review` with a sticky live-preview pane. Actual `app/pages/publish/new.vue` is a 202-line single-form upload.
- **Filter drawer pickers self-marked "deferred"** in `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue:2-5`: DeptPicker, TagDrawer, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker. Breaks locked decision #3 (Mode B drawer-style multi-row filters).
- **`app/components/ui/` empty.** Reka UI is in `package.json` but no shadcn-vue primitives are wrapped.
- **Detail-page sub-components missing**: ExtHero, ExtAboutCard, ExtTabs (README/Manifest/Permissions tabs), ExtRelatedList, InstallCommand, ShareButton, SaveButton. The Nuxt detail page is 158 lines and inlines a stripped subset (vs 386 lines + 7 components in Next.js).
- **OG images not wired** — plan §13 specifies `@nuxtjs/og-image`; not in `nuxt.config.ts`.
- **Playwright coverage thin** — 1 spec (`theme-no-flash`) vs 3 in Next.js (browse, detail, navigation).

Intended outcome: close all the above in a single PR, organised as phases P14–P20 of `docs/plan.md`. After merge the original `../agentcenter/` tree can be deleted.

## Delivery strategy

- **One feature branch**, one PR — provisional name `feat/finish-rewrite`.
- **One or several commits per phase**, scoped `feat(pN-…): …` per Conventional Commits. This is a **deliberate deviation** from CLAUDE.md "commit on each step"; the PR description must call it out.
- **Per-commit gate**: `bun run typecheck` passes.
- **Per-PR gate** (runs once at the end): `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.
- **Per-phase checkpoint**: pause for explicit human go-ahead before starting the next phase's first commit (CLAUDE.md Workflow §).
- `docs/plan.md` is updated **in the same commit** as any binding-decision change (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo).
- **No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.

## Phases

### P14 — CLI port — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p14-cli): import cli/ from original repo, point default base URL at new deployment`
- `docs(p14-cli): note CLI lives in-repo, update plan.md §1 + CLAUDE.md #8`

Files: new `cli/**` (copy of `../agentcenter/cli/`), `docs/plan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Smoke: `bun --cwd cli build && cli/dist/agentcenter --help`.

### P15 — UI primitives — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p15-ui): add components.json, port shadcn-vue primitives`

Use `npx shadcn-vue add` for: Button, Input, Label, Textarea, Select, Popover, Dialog, Sheet, Tabs, Checkbox, Skeleton. These are mechanical wrappers around Reka UI (already a dependency).

Files: `components.json`, `app/components/ui/**`.

### P16 — Filter drawer pickers — 2–3 commits

- `feat(p16-filters): add facets endpoint + drawer picker components`
- `feat(p16-filters): wire pickers into FilterBar, remove deferred comment`
- `test(p16-filters): port picker + chip tests from Next.js`

Reuse: `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts`, `app/composables/useFilters.ts`. Port `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` → `server/utils/queries/facets.ts` + `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`.

Files: `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue`, `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (replace deferred block), tests under `app/components/filters/*.nuxt.test.ts`.

### P17 — Publish wizard + edit route — 3–4 commits

- `feat(p17-wizard): wizard state composable + live preview`
- `feat(p17-wizard): step components (Basics, Bundle, Listing, Review)`
- `feat(p17-wizard): assemble UploadWizard, rewrite publish/new.vue, add publish/[id]/edit.vue`
- `feat(p17-wizard): port DiscardButton + wizard-skeleton + dashboard-skeleton`

Rebuild forms in Vue with vee-validate + Zod (Zod schemas already in `shared/validators/manifest.ts`); do **not** try to port React Hook Form 1:1. Reuse existing `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts`. Add `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` for the edit/resume flow if not present.

Files: `app/components/publish/**` (new dir + wizard subtree), `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (rewrite), `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (new), possibly `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts`.

### P18 — Profile page — 2–4 commits

- `chore(p18-profile): port shared/validators/profile.ts + server queries`
- `feat(p18-profile): add profile internal endpoints (settings + sections data)`
- `feat(p18-profile): add profile components and pages/profile.vue`
- `test(p18-profile): profile component + query tests`

Reuse Next.js originals (`lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, 11 components under `components/profile/`) as design reference, rewrite in Vue. `shared/**` requires ≥95% line / 90% branch coverage — write tests in the same commit as the pure logic.

Files: `shared/validators/profile.ts`, `server/utils/queries/profile.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `app/components/profile/{ProfileHero,ProfileSettingsForm,SectionRail,SettingsTabLink,ComingSoon,SectionActivity,SectionDrafts,SectionInstalled,SectionPublished,SectionSaved}.vue`, `app/pages/profile.vue`.

### P19 — Detail-page sub-components — 1–2 commits

- `refactor(p19-detail): split [slug].vue into ExtHero/ExtAboutCard/ExtTabs/ExtRelatedList`
- `feat(p19-detail): add InstallCommand + ShareButton + SaveButton + tests`

Break the 158-line monolith at `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` into the same component shape as the Next.js original. ExtTabs covers README / Manifest / Permissions.

Files: `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue`, `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (slim down).

### P20 — Polish round 2 — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p20-polish): add @nuxtjs/og-image + OG image templates for root and extensions/[slug]`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright browse + detail + navigation specs`

Files: `nuxt.config.ts` (register module), `app/og/**` (per `@nuxtjs/og-image` convention), `tests/e2e/{browse,detail,navigation}.spec.ts`.

## Critical files (modified or created)

- New top-level: `cli/**` (P14), `components.json` (P15).
- New components: `app/components/ui/**` (P15), `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue` (P16), `app/components/publish/**` (P17), `app/components/profile/**` (P18), `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue` (P19).
- New pages: `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (P17), `app/pages/profile.vue` (P18).
- New shared: `shared/validators/profile.ts` (P18).
- New server: `server/utils/queries/{facets,profile}.ts`, `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` (P16/P17/P18).
- Rewritten: `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (P17), `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (P19), `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (P16).
- Config: `nuxt.config.ts` (P20).
- Docs: `docs/plan.md` (P14 + as needed), `CLAUDE.md` (P14).

## Reuse map (existing code to lean on, not duplicate)

- `shared/validators/manifest.ts` — Zod schemas for wizard forms (P17).
- `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts` — picker option sources (P16).
- `app/composables/useFilters.ts` — picker selection state (P16).
- `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts` — wizard backend (P17, already wired).
- `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts`, `extension-detail.ts`, `collections.ts` — profile section data sources (P18).
- `server/utils/auth.ts`, `app/composables/useAuth.ts` — session for profile + edit gates.
- Reka UI + shadcn-vue (in `package.json`) for all UI primitives (P15).
- `lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, `components/publish/wizard/**`, `components/profile/**` in `../agentcenter/` — design reference only; rewrite in Vue.

## Verification

- **Per-commit**: `bun run typecheck`.
- **Per-phase smoke**:
  - P14: `cli/dist/agentcenter login && agentcenter install <slug>` against a local Nitro server.
  - P15: render each primitive on a scratch route or via existing pages.
  - P16: open `/en/extensions`, drawer opens, dept/tag/creator/publisher pickers narrow results, URL query updates.
  - P17: sign in → `/en/publish/new` → 4 steps → preview updates live → submit → see entry on `/en/publish` → open `/en/publish/[id]/edit` → discard works.
  - P18: `/en/profile` loads, settings form submits, all 5 section tabs render real data.
  - P19: `/en/extensions/[slug]` shows hero + about side panel + 3 tabs + related list, install/share/save work.
  - P20: OG image renders for root and a detail page; Playwright specs pass.
- **Pre-PR**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage). Coverage thresholds: `shared/**` ≥ 95% lines / 90% branches, `app/composables/**` ≥ 90%, `server/utils/**` ≥ 80%, `server/api/**` ≥ 70%, `app/components/**` ≥ 60% overall (≥ 80% for interactive).
- **Pre-PR**: Playwright e2e green locally.
- **PR description**: explicitly note the per-phase (not per-step) commit cadence as a one-time deviation from CLAUDE.md §Workflow.

- **`app/components/ui/` empty.** Reka UI is in `package.json` but no shadcn-vue primitives are wrapped.
- **Detail-page sub-components missing**: ExtHero, ExtAboutCard, ExtTabs (README/Manifest/Permissions tabs), ExtRelatedList, InstallCommand, ShareButton, SaveButton. The Nuxt detail page is 158 lines and inlines a stripped subset (vs 386 lines + 7 components in Next.js).
- **OG images not wired** — plan §13 specifies `@nuxtjs/og-image`; not in `nuxt.config.ts`.
- **Playwright coverage thin** — 1 spec (`theme-no-flash`) vs 3 in Next.js (browse, detail, navigation).

Intended outcome: close all the above in a single PR, organised as phases P14–P20 of `docs/plan.md`. After merge the original `../agentcenter/` tree can be deleted.

## Delivery strategy

- **One feature branch**, one PR — provisional name `feat/finish-rewrite`.
- **One or several commits per phase**, scoped `feat(pN-…): …` per Conventional Commits. This is a **deliberate deviation** from CLAUDE.md "commit on each step"; the PR description must call it out.
- **Per-commit gate**: `bun run typecheck` passes.
- **Per-PR gate** (runs once at the end): `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.
- **Per-phase checkpoint**: pause for explicit human go-ahead before starting the next phase's first commit (CLAUDE.md Workflow §).
- `docs/plan.md` is updated **in the same commit** as any binding-decision change (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo).
- **No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.

## Phases

### P14 — CLI port — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p14-cli): import cli/ from original repo, point default base URL at new deployment`
- `docs(p14-cli): note CLI lives in-repo, update plan.md §1 + CLAUDE.md #8`

Files: new `cli/**` (copy of `../agentcenter/cli/`), `docs/plan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Smoke: `bun --cwd cli build && cli/dist/agentcenter --help`.

### P15 — UI primitives — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p15-ui): add components.json, port shadcn-vue primitives`

Use `npx shadcn-vue add` for: Button, Input, Label, Textarea, Select, Popover, Dialog, Sheet, Tabs, Checkbox, Skeleton. These are mechanical wrappers around Reka UI (already a dependency).

Files: `components.json`, `app/components/ui/**`.

### P16 — Filter drawer pickers — 2–3 commits

- `feat(p16-filters): add facets endpoint + drawer picker components`
- `feat(p16-filters): wire pickers into FilterBar, remove deferred comment`
- `test(p16-filters): port picker + chip tests from Next.js`

Reuse: `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts`, `app/composables/useFilters.ts`. Port `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` → `server/utils/queries/facets.ts` + `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`.

Files: `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue`, `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (replace deferred block), tests under `app/components/filters/*.nuxt.test.ts`.

### P17 — Publish wizard + edit route — 3–4 commits

- `feat(p17-wizard): wizard state composable + live preview`
- `feat(p17-wizard): step components (Basics, Bundle, Listing, Review)`
- `feat(p17-wizard): assemble UploadWizard, rewrite publish/new.vue, add publish/[id]/edit.vue`
- `feat(p17-wizard): port DiscardButton + wizard-skeleton + dashboard-skeleton`

Rebuild forms in Vue with vee-validate + Zod (Zod schemas already in `shared/validators/manifest.ts`); do **not** try to port React Hook Form 1:1. Reuse existing `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts`. Add `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` for the edit/resume flow if not present.

Files: `app/components/publish/**` (new dir + wizard subtree), `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (rewrite), `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (new), possibly `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts`.

- `test(p18-profile): profile component + query tests`

Reuse Next.js originals (`lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, 11 components under `components/profile/`) as design reference, rewrite in Vue. `shared/**` requires ≥95% line / 90% branch coverage — write tests in the same commit as the pure logic.

### P20 — Polish round 2 — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p20-polish): add @nuxtjs/og-image + OG image templates for root and extensions/[slug]`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright browse + detail + navigation specs`

- New shared: `shared/validators/profile.ts` (P18).
- New server: `server/utils/queries/{facets,profile}.ts`, `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` (P16/P17/P18).

message.content: if you think one PR is too big, you can sperate it, but not too much. if one is ok, we can choose one PR for all

content.text:
Fair — 3–5k LOC in one PR is genuinely hard to review. My recommendation: **3 PRs**, split along natural seams.

- **PR 1 — foundation (P14 + P15)**: CLI port + UI primitives. ~500 LOC, mostly mechanical (copy + shadcn-vue generators). Low review risk; unblocks everything else.
- **PR 2 — write paths (P16 + P17)**: filter drawer pickers + publish wizard + edit route. ~2k LOC. Both are interactive forms that consume PR 1's primitives. One coherent review theme: "things the user submits".
- **PR 3 — read paths + polish (P18 + P19 + P20)**: profile page + detail-page split + OG images + e2e specs. ~1.5–2k LOC. Theme: "things the user reads".

Each PR is independently shippable, ~1–2k LOC, and reviewable in one sitting. Per-phase commits stay within each PR.

Let me update the plan file to reflect the 3-PR strategy.

## Context

- **`/profile` route + 11 components missing.** Plan.md:194 lists this as P0. `shared/validators/profile.ts` was not ported either.

## Delivery strategy

- **Per-commit gate**: `bun run typecheck` passes.
- **Per-PR gate** (runs once at the end): `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.

## Phases

### P14 — CLI port — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p14-cli): import cli/ from original repo, point default base URL at new deployment`

### P15 — UI primitives — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p15-ui): add components.json, port shadcn-vue primitives`

Use `npx shadcn-vue add` for: Button, Input, Label, Textarea, Select, Popover, Dialog, Sheet, Tabs, Checkbox, Skeleton. These are mechanical wrappers around Reka UI (already a dependency).

Files: `components.json`, `app/components/ui/**`.

### P16 — Filter drawer pickers — 2–3 commits

- `feat(p16-filters): add facets endpoint + drawer picker components`
- `feat(p16-filters): wire pickers into FilterBar, remove deferred comment`
- `test(p16-filters): port picker + chip tests from Next.js`

Reuse: `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts`, `app/composables/useFilters.ts`. Port `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` → `server/utils/queries/facets.ts` + `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`.

Files: `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue`, `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (replace deferred block), tests under `app/components/filters/*.nuxt.test.ts`.

### P17 — Publish wizard + edit route — 3–4 commits

- `feat(p17-wizard): wizard state composable + live preview`
- `feat(p17-wizard): step components (Basics, Bundle, Listing, Review)`
- `feat(p17-wizard): assemble UploadWizard, rewrite publish/new.vue, add publish/[id]/edit.vue`
- `feat(p17-wizard): port DiscardButton + wizard-skeleton + dashboard-skeleton`

Rebuild forms in Vue with vee-validate + Zod (Zod schemas already in `shared/validators/manifest.ts`); do **not** try to port React Hook Form 1:1. Reuse existing `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts`. Add `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` for the edit/resume flow if not present.

### P18 — Profile page — 2–4 commits

- `chore(p18-profile): port shared/validators/profile.ts + server queries`
- `feat(p18-profile): add profile internal endpoints (settings + sections data)`

- `test(p18-profile): profile component + query tests`

Reuse Next.js originals (`lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, 11 components under `components/profile/`) as design reference, rewrite in Vue. `shared/**` requires ≥95% line / 90% branch coverage — write tests in the same commit as the pure logic.

Files: `shared/validators/profile.ts`, `server/utils/queries/profile.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `app/components/profile/{ProfileHero,ProfileSettingsForm,SectionRail,SettingsTabLink,ComingSoon,SectionActivity,SectionDrafts,SectionInstalled,SectionPublished,SectionSaved}.vue`, `app/pages/profile.vue`.

### P19 — Detail-page sub-components — 1–2 commits

- `refactor(p19-detail): split [slug].vue into ExtHero/ExtAboutCard/ExtTabs/ExtRelatedList`
- `feat(p19-detail): add InstallCommand + ShareButton + SaveButton + tests`

Break the 158-line monolith at `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` into the same component shape as the Next.js original. ExtTabs covers README / Manifest / Permissions.

Files: `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue`, `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (slim down).

### P20 — Polish round 2 — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p20-polish): add @nuxtjs/og-image + OG image templates for root and extensions/[slug]`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright browse + detail + navigation specs`

Files: `nuxt.config.ts` (register module), `app/og/**` (per `@nuxtjs/og-image` convention), `tests/e2e/{browse,detail,navigation}.spec.ts`.

## Critical files (modified or created)

- New top-level: `cli/**` (P14), `components.json` (P15).
- New components: `app/components/ui/**` (P15), `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue` (P16), `app/components/publish/**` (P17), `app/components/profile/**` (P18), `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue` (P19).
- New pages: `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (P17), `app/pages/profile.vue` (P18).
- New shared: `shared/validators/profile.ts` (P18).
- New server: `server/utils/queries/{facets,profile}.ts`, `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` (P16/P17/P18).
- Rewritten: `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (P17), `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (P19), `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (P16).
- Config: `nuxt.config.ts` (P20).
- Docs: `docs/plan.md` (P14 + as needed), `CLAUDE.md` (P14).

## Reuse map (existing code to lean on, not duplicate)

- `shared/validators/manifest.ts` — Zod schemas for wizard forms (P17).
- `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts` — picker option sources (P16).
- `app/composables/useFilters.ts` — picker selection state (P16).
- `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts` — wizard backend (P17, already wired).
- `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts`, `extension-detail.ts`, `collections.ts` — profile section data sources (P18).
- `server/utils/auth.ts`, `app/composables/useAuth.ts` — session for profile + edit gates.
- Reka UI + shadcn-vue (in `package.json`) for all UI primitives (P15).
- `lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, `components/publish/wizard/**`, `components/profile/**` in `../agentcenter/` — design reference only; rewrite in Vue.

## Verification

- **Per-commit**: `bun run typecheck`.
- **Per-phase smoke**:
  - P14: `cli/dist/agentcenter login && agentcenter install <slug>` against a local Nitro server.
  - P15: render each primitive on a scratch route or via existing pages.
  - P16: open `/en/extensions`, drawer opens, dept/tag/creator/publisher pickers narrow results, URL query updates.
  - P17: sign in → `/en/publish/new` → 4 steps → preview updates live → submit → see entry on `/en/publish` → open `/en/publish/[id]/edit` → discard works.
  - P18: `/en/profile` loads, settings form submits, all 5 section tabs render real data.
  - P19: `/en/extensions/[slug]` shows hero + about side panel + 3 tabs + related list, install/share/save work.
  - P20: OG image renders for root and a detail page; Playwright specs pass.
- **Pre-PR**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage). Coverage thresholds: `shared/**` ≥ 95% lines / 90% branches, `app/composables/**` ≥ 90%, `server/utils/**` ≥ 80%, `server/api/**` ≥ 70%, `app/components/**` ≥ 60% overall (≥ 80% for interactive).
- **Pre-PR**: Playwright e2e green locally.
- **PR description**: explicitly note the per-phase (not per-step) commit cadence as a one-time deviation from CLAUDE.md §Workflow.

Intended outcome: close all the above across phases P14–P20 of `docs/plan.md`, delivered as **3 PRs** along natural seams. After all three merge, the original `../agentcenter/` tree can be deleted.

## Delivery strategy

**Three PRs, each shippable independently.** Splitting along seams keeps each review ~1–2k LOC and gives natural rollback boundaries.

- **PR 1 — foundation** (branch `feat/foundation`): P14 (CLI port) + P15 (UI primitives). ~500 LOC. Mostly mechanical: file copy + shadcn-vue generators. No UX visible yet. Lands first because PR 2 and PR 3 depend on it.
- **PR 2 — write paths** (branch `feat/filters-and-wizard`, off `main` after PR 1 merges): P16 (filter drawer pickers) + P17 (publish wizard + edit route). ~2k LOC. Theme: forms the user submits. Both consume PR 1's primitives.
- **PR 3 — read paths + polish** (branch `feat/profile-and-polish`, off `main` after PR 2 merges): P18 (profile) + P19 (detail-page split) + P20 (OG images + e2e specs). ~1.5–2k LOC. Theme: surfaces the user reads.

- **One or several commits per phase**, scoped `feat(pN-…): …` per Conventional Commits. Deliberate deviation from CLAUDE.md "commit on each step"; each PR description calls it out.

- **Per-PR gate**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.
- **Per-phase checkpoint**: pause for explicit human go-ahead before starting the next phase's first commit (CLAUDE.md Workflow §).
- **Per-PR checkpoint**: after each PR merges, pause for human go-ahead before opening the next branch.
- `docs/plan.md` is updated **in the same commit** as any binding-decision change (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo).
- **No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.

input.plan:
# Finish the Nuxt rewrite — P14–P20 across 3 PRs

## Context

This repo (`agentcenter-nuxt/`) is the Nuxt 4 rewrite of `../agentcenter/`. Phases P0–P13 of `docs/plan.md` shipped, but several routes and components that the plan marked **P0** were skipped or scoped down. The user is abandoning the original repo, so the remaining gaps must land here before the old tree can be deleted.

Confirmed gaps (verified against both trees on 2026-05-13):

- **`cli/` directory missing.** Plan §1 has it living here; today it only exists in `../agentcenter/cli/`. CLAUDE.md item #8 says it carries over unchanged — only the base URL flips.
- **`/profile` route + 11 components missing.** Plan.md:194 lists this as P0. `shared/validators/profile.ts` was not ported either.
- **`/publish/[id]/edit` route missing.** Plan.md:192. Drafts created via `publish/new` cannot be resumed.
- **4-step publish wizard with live preview missing.** Plan §10 (plan.md:294) specifies `Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review` with a sticky live-preview pane. Actual `app/pages/publish/new.vue` is a 202-line single-form upload.
- **Filter drawer pickers self-marked "deferred"** in `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue:2-5`: DeptPicker, TagDrawer, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker. Breaks locked decision #3 (Mode B drawer-style multi-row filters).
- **`app/components/ui/` empty.** Reka UI is in `package.json` but no shadcn-vue primitives are wrapped.
- **Detail-page sub-components missing**: ExtHero, ExtAboutCard, ExtTabs (README/Manifest/Permissions tabs), ExtRelatedList, InstallCommand, ShareButton, SaveButton. The Nuxt detail page is 158 lines and inlines a stripped subset (vs 386 lines + 7 components in Next.js).
- **OG images not wired** — plan §13 specifies `@nuxtjs/og-image`; not in `nuxt.config.ts`.
- **Playwright coverage thin** — 1 spec (`theme-no-flash`) vs 3 in Next.js (browse, detail, navigation).

Intended outcome: close all the above across phases P14–P20 of `docs/plan.md`, delivered as **3 PRs** along natural seams. After all three merge, the original `../agentcenter/` tree can be deleted.

## Delivery strategy

**Three PRs, each shippable independently.** Splitting along seams keeps each review ~1–2k LOC and gives natural rollback boundaries.

- **PR 1 — foundation** (branch `feat/foundation`): P14 (CLI port) + P15 (UI primitives). ~500 LOC. Mostly mechanical: file copy + shadcn-vue generators. No UX visible yet. Lands first because PR 2 and PR 3 depend on it.
- **PR 2 — write paths** (branch `feat/filters-and-wizard`, off `main` after PR 1 merges): P16 (filter drawer pickers) + P17 (publish wizard + edit route). ~2k LOC. Theme: forms the user submits. Both consume PR 1's primitives.
- **PR 3 — read paths + polish** (branch `feat/profile-and-polish`, off `main` after PR 2 merges): P18 (profile) + P19 (detail-page split) + P20 (OG images + e2e specs). ~1.5–2k LOC. Theme: surfaces the user reads.

Conventions inside each PR:

- **One or several commits per phase**, scoped `feat(pN-…): …` per Conventional Commits. Deliberate deviation from CLAUDE.md "commit on each step"; each PR description calls it out.
- **Per-commit gate**: `bun run typecheck` passes.
- **Per-PR gate**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.
- **Per-phase checkpoint**: pause for explicit human go-ahead before starting the next phase's first commit (CLAUDE.md Workflow §).
- **Per-PR checkpoint**: after each PR merges, pause for human go-ahead before opening the next branch.
- `docs/plan.md` is updated **in the same commit** as any binding-decision change (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo).
- **No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.

## Phases

### PR 1 — foundation

### P14 — CLI port — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p14-cli): import cli/ from original repo, point default base URL at new deployment`
- `docs(p14-cli): note CLI lives in-repo, update plan.md §1 + CLAUDE.md #8`

Files: new `cli/**` (copy of `../agentcenter/cli/`), `docs/plan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Smoke: `bun --cwd cli build && cli/dist/agentcenter --help`.

### P15 — UI primitives — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p15-ui): add components.json, port shadcn-vue primitives`

Use `npx shadcn-vue add` for: Button, Input, Label, Textarea, Select, Popover, Dialog, Sheet, Tabs, Checkbox, Skeleton. These are mechanical wrappers around Reka UI (already a dependency).

- `feat(p17-wizard): wizard state composable + live preview`

- `feat(p17-wizard): assemble UploadWizard, rewrite publish/new.vue, add publish/[id]/edit.vue`
- `feat(p17-wizard): port DiscardButton + wizard-skeleton + dashboard-skeleton`

Rebuild forms in Vue with vee-validate + Zod (Zod schemas already in `shared/validators/manifest.ts`); do **not** try to port React Hook Form 1:1. Reuse existing `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts`. Add `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` for the edit/resume flow if not present.

Files: `app/components/publish/**` (new dir + wizard subtree), `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (rewrite), `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (new), possibly `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts`.

---

- `chore(p18-profile): port shared/validators/profile.ts + server queries`

- `test(p18-profile): profile component + query tests`

Reuse Next.js originals (`lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, 11 components under `components/profile/`) as design reference, rewrite in Vue. `shared/**` requires ≥95% line / 90% branch coverage — write tests in the same commit as the pure logic.

Files: `shared/validators/profile.ts`, `server/utils/queries/profile.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `app/components/profile/{ProfileHero,ProfileSettingsForm,SectionRail,SettingsTabLink,ComingSoon,SectionActivity,SectionDrafts,SectionInstalled,SectionPublished,SectionSaved}.vue`, `app/pages/profile.vue`.

### P19 — Detail-page sub-components — 1–2 commits

- `refactor(p19-detail): split [slug].vue into ExtHero/ExtAboutCard/ExtTabs/ExtRelatedList`
- `feat(p19-detail): add InstallCommand + ShareButton + SaveButton + tests`

Break the 158-line monolith at `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` into the same component shape as the Next.js original. ExtTabs covers README / Manifest / Permissions.

Files: `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue`, `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (slim down).

### P20 — Polish round 2 — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p20-polish): add @nuxtjs/og-image + OG image templates for root and extensions/[slug]`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright browse + detail + navigation specs`

Files: `nuxt.config.ts` (register module), `app/og/**` (per `@nuxtjs/og-image` convention), `tests/e2e/{browse,detail,navigation}.spec.ts`.

## Critical files (modified or created)

- New top-level: `cli/**` (P14), `components.json` (P15).
- New components: `app/components/ui/**` (P15), `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue` (P16), `app/components/publish/**` (P17), `app/components/profile/**` (P18), `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue` (P19).
- New pages: `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (P17), `app/pages/profile.vue` (P18).
- New shared: `shared/validators/profile.ts` (P18).
- New server: `server/utils/queries/{facets,profile}.ts`, `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` (P16/P17/P18).
- Rewritten: `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (P17), `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (P19), `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (P16).
- Config: `nuxt.config.ts` (P20).
- Docs: `docs/plan.md` (P14 + as needed), `CLAUDE.md` (P14).

## Reuse map (existing code to lean on, not duplicate)

- `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts` — picker option sources (P16).

content.content:
1	# AgentCenter
2	
3	Internal-flavored marketplace for AI agent extensions: Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins. Bilingual (EN/ZH) from day one. See `docs/plan.md` for the full implementation plan and milestone schedule.
4	
5	## Tech stack
6	
7	- TypeScript, Next.js 16 (App Router, RSC, Turbopack dev), Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui + Radix
8	  - Next 16 has breaking changes from earlier versions; consult `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` and the project's top-level `AGENTS.md` before writing Next-specific code.
9	- Bun for local dev and CLI build; Vercel + Node runtime for the web app in prod
10	- PostgreSQL (Neon hosted) with Drizzle ORM
11	- Better Auth (cookie sessions, Drizzle adapter)
12	- Cloudflare R2 for bundle storage
13	- Inngest for background jobs (scan, index, publish)
14	- next-intl for i18n; locales `en` (default) + `zh`, always-prefixed URLs (`/en/...`, `/zh/...`)
15	- Fonts via `next/font`: Inter (UI), Fraunces (display, italic), JetBrains Mono (tags/counts)
16	- Postgres FTS + `pg_trgm` for search; Meilisearch deferred
17	
18	## Locked product decisions
19	
20	1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** for v1.
21	2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark. Mono Clean dropped.
22	3. **Filter UI**: Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only. Modes A/C in the prototype were comparison scaffolding.
23	4. **Sign-up**: open. SSO/invite-only later if needed.
24	5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
25	6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with `react-markdown` + `rehype-sanitize`. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.
26	7. **Install button**: triggers real install via the Agent CLI. Web records install events; the CLI does the work.
27	8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm (Bun-built binary).
28	9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.

file.content:
# AgentCenter

Internal-flavored marketplace for AI agent extensions: Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins. Bilingual (EN/ZH) from day one. See `docs/plan.md` for the full implementation plan and milestone schedule.

## Tech stack

- TypeScript, Next.js 16 (App Router, RSC, Turbopack dev), Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui + Radix
  - Next 16 has breaking changes from earlier versions; consult `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` and the project's top-level `AGENTS.md` before writing Next-specific code.
- Bun for local dev and CLI build; Vercel + Node runtime for the web app in prod
- PostgreSQL (Neon hosted) with Drizzle ORM
- Better Auth (cookie sessions, Drizzle adapter)
- Cloudflare R2 for bundle storage
- Inngest for background jobs (scan, index, publish)
- next-intl for i18n; locales `en` (default) + `zh`, always-prefixed URLs (`/en/...`, `/zh/...`)
- Fonts via `next/font`: Inter (UI), Fraunces (display, italic), JetBrains Mono (tags/counts)
- Postgres FTS + `pg_trgm` for search; Meilisearch deferred

## Locked product decisions

1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** for v1.
2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark. Mono Clean dropped.
3. **Filter UI**: Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only. Modes A/C in the prototype were comparison scaffolding.
4. **Sign-up**: open. SSO/invite-only later if needed.
5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with `react-markdown` + `rehype-sanitize`. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.
7. **Install button**: triggers real install via the Agent CLI. Web records install events; the CLI does the work.
8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm (Bun-built binary).
9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.
10. **Dynamic content i18n**: column-per-language (`name` + `nameZh`, `description` + `descriptionZh`). `tags` table carries `labelEn` + `labelZh`.

## Workflow

- **Commit per coherent unit** — roughly one per phase, split when natural. Conventional-commits style with phase scope: `feat(p2-db): drizzle schema + seed data`.
- Before each commit, surface a diff summary and proposed commit message. Wait for explicit go-ahead before running `git commit`.
- At the end of each phase, pause for an explicit checkpoint before starting the next phase.
- No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`. Corrective commits over history rewrites.
- Always work on a feature branch and open a PR — never commit to `main` directly. CI (lint, typecheck, tests) and CodeRabbit run on every PR.
- When a binding decision changes, update `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.

## Project structure (target)

See `docs/plan.md` §1 for the full tree. High-level layout:

- `app/[locale]/...` — App Router pages, locale-segmented
- `app/api/...` — Better Auth handler, R2 presigned upload, install events, Inngest webhook, public registry API (`/api/v1/...`) used by the CLI
- `components/{ui,layout,extension,filters,publish}` — UI components
- `lib/{db,auth,i18n,storage,jobs,search,validators}` — server-side infra
- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary, ships in Phase 9 (agent-agnostic, Claude-first defaults)
- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

## Design references

The original prototype lives outside this repo at `/tmp/agentcenter-design/` (extracted from a Claude Design handoff bundle). Key files:

- `agentcenter/project/AgentCenter v2.html` — the primary design (1641 lines, Editorial Ivory aesthetic, Mode B filters)
- `agentcenter/chats/chat1.md` — full design conversation, including the iteration history that produced the locked decisions

These are reference-only. Production components recreate the design in React/Tailwind, not by copying the prototype's inline styles.

You're right. The original CLAUDE.md is 59 lines; the Nuxt one is 88 lines, and the Workflow section alone roughly tripled. Let me map exactly what got added:

**Original Workflow (6 bullets):**
- Commit per coherent unit — **roughly one per phase, split when natural**
- Surface diff + commit message before commit
- Pause at end of each phase
- No `--amend` / force-push / `--no-verify`
- Always feature branch + PR
- Update `docs/plan.md` in same commit when binding decisions change

**Nuxt Workflow (~15 bullets) added:**
- "Commit on each step. A phase is many commits; one per checklist item or TodoWrite checkbox."
- "Always-split boundaries: schema vs. consumers, refactor vs. feature, generated code vs. source, dep install vs. first use"
- "Per-commit gate" vs "Per-PR gate" distinction
- "Breaking changes to /api/v1 require ! after the scope and a BREAKING CHANGE: footer"
- "No co-author trailers"
- "Merge strategy: PRs land as merge commits, not squashed and not rebased"
- "PR rules: PR title = Conventional Commit summary"
- "Reviewer-requested changes go in as new commits, not force-push"
- "Hooks (installed in P0): commit-msg runs commitlint; pre-commit runs lint-staged; pre-push runs bun run validate"

**Also new in Nuxt CLAUDE.md** — an entire `## Locked technical decisions` section (~12 bullets) that wasn't in the original at all: deployment target, auth choice, DB driver, storage default, background jobs, public API contract, server-actions-to-endpoints, validate pipeline, no-DB-mocking, coverage thresholds, TypeScript strict extras, layer boundaries, no barrel files, untested shared not landable.

**Two observations:**

1. **Our proposed strategy is actually the original's default.** "Commit per coherent unit — roughly one per phase, split when natural" is exactly what you asked for. The framing of "deliberate deviation" in my plan was wrong; it's only a deviation from the *Nuxt* rule, which itself drifted from the original. I'll remove the deviation framing from the plan.

2. **The Nuxt CLAUDE.md has accreted prescription that probably belongs in `docs/plan.md`** (coverage thresholds, hook details, layer boundaries — all already documented there). CLAUDE.md should be the always-loaded short-form context; the details should live in the doc the agent consults.

Suggested CLAUDE.md trim (separate change, could be its own small PR or rolled into PR 1):
- Workflow section back to 6–8 bullets, mirroring the original
- Drop `## Locked technical decisions` (it's a `docs/plan.md` thing)
- Keep: tech stack, locked **product** decisions, project structure, workflow basics, design references

Let me update the plan file to (a) remove the "deviation" framing and (b) add a P14a "trim CLAUDE.md" mini-phase to PR 1.

input.plan:
# Finish the Nuxt rewrite — P14–P20 across 3 PRs

## Context

This repo (`agentcenter-nuxt/`) is the Nuxt 4 rewrite of `../agentcenter/`. Phases P0–P13 of `docs/plan.md` shipped, but several routes and components that the plan marked **P0** were skipped or scoped down. The user is abandoning the original repo, so the remaining gaps must land here before the old tree can be deleted.

Confirmed gaps (verified against both trees on 2026-05-13):

- **`cli/` directory missing.** Plan §1 has it living here; today it only exists in `../agentcenter/cli/`. CLAUDE.md item #8 says it carries over unchanged — only the base URL flips.
- **`/profile` route + 11 components missing.** Plan.md:194 lists this as P0. `shared/validators/profile.ts` was not ported either.
- **`/publish/[id]/edit` route missing.** Plan.md:192. Drafts created via `publish/new` cannot be resumed.
- **4-step publish wizard with live preview missing.** Plan §10 (plan.md:294) specifies `Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review` with a sticky live-preview pane. Actual `app/pages/publish/new.vue` is a 202-line single-form upload.
- **Filter drawer pickers self-marked "deferred"** in `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue:2-5`: DeptPicker, TagDrawer, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker. Breaks locked decision #3 (Mode B drawer-style multi-row filters).
- **`app/components/ui/` empty.** Reka UI is in `package.json` but no shadcn-vue primitives are wrapped.
- **Detail-page sub-components missing**: ExtHero, ExtAboutCard, ExtTabs (README/Manifest/Permissions tabs), ExtRelatedList, InstallCommand, ShareButton, SaveButton. The Nuxt detail page is 158 lines and inlines a stripped subset (vs 386 lines + 7 components in Next.js).
- **OG images not wired** — plan §13 specifies `@nuxtjs/og-image`; not in `nuxt.config.ts`.
- **Playwright coverage thin** — 1 spec (`theme-no-flash`) vs 3 in Next.js (browse, detail, navigation).

Intended outcome: close all the above across phases P14–P20 of `docs/plan.md`, delivered as **3 PRs** along natural seams. After all three merge, the original `../agentcenter/` tree can be deleted.

## Delivery strategy

**Three PRs, each shippable independently.** Splitting along seams keeps each review ~1–2k LOC and gives natural rollback boundaries.

- **PR 1 — foundation** (branch `feat/foundation`): P14a (trim CLAUDE.md) + P14 (CLI port) + P15 (UI primitives). ~500 LOC. Mostly mechanical: docs trim + file copy + shadcn-vue generators. No UX visible yet. Lands first because PR 2 and PR 3 depend on it.
- **PR 2 — write paths** (branch `feat/filters-and-wizard`, off `main` after PR 1 merges): P16 (filter drawer pickers) + P17 (publish wizard + edit route). ~2k LOC. Theme: forms the user submits. Both consume PR 1's primitives.
- **PR 3 — read paths + polish** (branch `feat/profile-and-polish`, off `main` after PR 2 merges): P18 (profile) + P19 (detail-page split) + P20 (OG images + e2e specs). ~1.5–2k LOC. Theme: surfaces the user reads.

Conventions inside each PR:

- **One or several commits per phase**, scoped `feat(pN-…): …` per Conventional Commits. Matches the original repo's CLAUDE.md ("Commit per coherent unit — roughly one per phase, split when natural"). The current Nuxt CLAUDE.md's stricter "commit on each step" rule is being trimmed back in P14a (see below).
- **Per-commit gate**: `bun run typecheck` passes.
- **Per-PR gate**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.
- **Per-phase checkpoint**: pause for explicit human go-ahead before starting the next phase's first commit (CLAUDE.md Workflow §).
- **Per-PR checkpoint**: after each PR merges, pause for human go-ahead before opening the next branch.
- `docs/plan.md` is updated **in the same commit** as any binding-decision change (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo).
- **No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.

## Phases

### PR 1 — foundation

### P14a — Trim CLAUDE.md — 1 commit

The Nuxt CLAUDE.md has drifted from the original — 88 lines vs 59, with prescription that mostly duplicates `docs/plan.md` (coverage thresholds, hook details, layer boundaries, locked technical decisions, fine-grained commit rules). CLAUDE.md should be always-loaded short-form context; details belong in the doc the agent consults.

- `docs(p14a-claude): trim CLAUDE.md to short-form, point to docs/plan.md for details`

What to keep: tech stack, locked **product** decisions (10), project structure summary, workflow basics (~6 bullets matching the original), design references / pointers to plan.md.

What to drop or relocate to `docs/plan.md`: the entire `## Locked technical decisions` block, coverage thresholds, hook details, layer-boundary specifics, "commit on each step" sub-rules, merge strategy enumeration.

Files: `CLAUDE.md` (rewrite), `docs/plan.md` (absorb anything not already there).

### P14 — CLI port — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p14-cli): import cli/ from original repo, point default base URL at new deployment`
- `docs(p14-cli): note CLI lives in-repo, update plan.md §1 + CLAUDE.md #8`

Files: new `cli/**` (copy of `../agentcenter/cli/`), `docs/plan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Smoke: `bun --cwd cli build && cli/dist/agentcenter --help`.

### P15 — UI primitives — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p15-ui): add components.json, port shadcn-vue primitives`

Use `npx shadcn-vue add` for: Button, Input, Label, Textarea, Select, Popover, Dialog, Sheet, Tabs, Checkbox, Skeleton. These are mechanical wrappers around Reka UI (already a dependency).

Files: `components.json`, `app/components/ui/**`.

---

### PR 2 — write paths

### P16 — Filter drawer pickers — 2–3 commits

- `feat(p16-filters): add facets endpoint + drawer picker components`
- `feat(p16-filters): wire pickers into FilterBar, remove deferred comment`
- `test(p16-filters): port picker + chip tests from Next.js`

Reuse: `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts`, `app/composables/useFilters.ts`. Port `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` → `server/utils/queries/facets.ts` + `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`.

Files: `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue`, `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (replace deferred block), tests under `app/components/filters/*.nuxt.test.ts`.

### P17 — Publish wizard + edit route — 3–4 commits

- `feat(p17-wizard): wizard state composable + live preview`
- `feat(p17-wizard): step components (Basics, Bundle, Listing, Review)`
- `feat(p17-wizard): assemble UploadWizard, rewrite publish/new.vue, add publish/[id]/edit.vue`
- `feat(p17-wizard): port DiscardButton + wizard-skeleton + dashboard-skeleton`

Rebuild forms in Vue with vee-validate + Zod (Zod schemas already in `shared/validators/manifest.ts`); do **not** try to port React Hook Form 1:1. Reuse existing `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts`. Add `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` for the edit/resume flow if not present.

Files: `app/components/publish/**` (new dir + wizard subtree), `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (rewrite), `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (new), possibly `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts`.

---

### PR 3 — read paths + polish

### P18 — Profile page — 2–4 commits

- `chore(p18-profile): port shared/validators/profile.ts + server queries`
- `feat(p18-profile): add profile internal endpoints (settings + sections data)`
- `feat(p18-profile): add profile components and pages/profile.vue`
- `test(p18-profile): profile component + query tests`

## Approved Plan:
# Finish the Nuxt rewrite — P14–P20 across 3 PRs

## Context

This repo (`agentcenter-nuxt/`) is the Nuxt 4 rewrite of `../agentcenter/`. Phases P0–P13 of `docs/plan.md` shipped, but several routes and components that the plan marked **P0** were skipped or scoped down. The user is abandoning the original repo, so the remaining gaps must land here before the old tree can be deleted.

Confirmed gaps (verified against both trees on 2026-05-13):

- **`cli/` directory missing.** Plan §1 has it living here; today it only exists in `../agentcenter/cli/`. CLAUDE.md item #8 says it carries over unchanged — only the base URL flips.
- **`/profile` route + 11 components missing.** Plan.md:194 lists this as P0. `shared/validators/profile.ts` was not ported either.
- **`/publish/[id]/edit` route missing.** Plan.md:192. Drafts created via `publish/new` cannot be resumed.
- **4-step publish wizard with live preview missing.** Plan §10 (plan.md:294) specifies `Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review` with a sticky live-preview pane. Actual `app/pages/publish/new.vue` is a 202-line single-form upload.
- **Filter drawer pickers self-marked "deferred"** in `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue:2-5`: DeptPicker, TagDrawer, CreatorPicker, PublisherPicker. Breaks locked decision #3 (Mode B drawer-style multi-row filters).
- **`app/components/ui/` empty.** Reka UI is in `package.json` but no shadcn-vue primitives are wrapped.
- **Detail-page sub-components missing**: ExtHero, ExtAboutCard, ExtTabs (README/Manifest/Permissions tabs), ExtRelatedList, InstallCommand, ShareButton, SaveButton. The Nuxt detail page is 158 lines and inlines a stripped subset (vs 386 lines + 7 components in Next.js).
- **OG images not wired** — plan §13 specifies `@nuxtjs/og-image`; not in `nuxt.config.ts`.
- **Playwright coverage thin** — 1 spec (`theme-no-flash`) vs 3 in Next.js (browse, detail, navigation).

Intended outcome: close all the above across phases P14–P20 of `docs/plan.md`, delivered as **3 PRs** along natural seams. After all three merge, the original `../agentcenter/` tree can be deleted.

## Delivery strategy

**Three PRs, each shippable independently.** Splitting along seams keeps each review ~1–2k LOC and gives natural rollback boundaries.

- **PR 1 — foundation** (branch `feat/foundation`): P14a (trim CLAUDE.md) + P14 (CLI port) + P15 (UI primitives). ~500 LOC. Mostly mechanical: docs trim + file copy + shadcn-vue generators. No UX visible yet. Lands first because PR 2 and PR 3 depend on it.
- **PR 2 — write paths** (branch `feat/filters-and-wizard`, off `main` after PR 1 merges): P16 (filter drawer pickers) + P17 (publish wizard + edit route). ~2k LOC. Theme: forms the user submits. Both consume PR 1's primitives.
- **PR 3 — read paths + polish** (branch `feat/profile-and-polish`, off `main` after PR 2 merges): P18 (profile) + P19 (detail-page split) + P20 (OG images + e2e specs). ~1.5–2k LOC. Theme: surfaces the user reads.

Conventions inside each PR:

- **One or several commits per phase**, scoped `feat(pN-…): …` per Conventional Commits. Matches the original repo's CLAUDE.md ("Commit per coherent unit — roughly one per phase, split when natural"). The current Nuxt CLAUDE.md's stricter "commit on each step" rule is being trimmed back in P14a (see below).
- **Per-commit gate**: `bun run typecheck` passes.
- **Per-PR gate**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage thresholds), Playwright e2e.
- **Per-phase checkpoint**: pause for explicit human go-ahead before starting the next phase's first commit (CLAUDE.md Workflow §).
- **Per-PR checkpoint**: after each PR merges, pause for human go-ahead before opening the next branch.
- `docs/plan.md` is updated **in the same commit** as any binding-decision change (e.g. CLI now lives in-repo).
- **No `--amend`, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.

## Phases

### PR 1 — foundation

### P14a — Trim CLAUDE.md — 1 commit

The Nuxt CLAUDE.md has drifted from the original — 88 lines vs 59, with prescription that mostly duplicates `docs/plan.md` (coverage thresholds, hook details, layer boundaries, locked technical decisions, fine-grained commit rules). CLAUDE.md should be always-loaded short-form context; details belong in the doc the agent consults.

What to keep: tech stack, locked **product** decisions (10), project structure summary, workflow basics (~6 bullets matching the original), design references / pointers to plan.md.

What to drop or relocate to `docs/plan.md`: the entire `## Locked technical decisions` block, coverage thresholds, hook details, layer-boundary specifics, "commit on each step" sub-rules, merge strategy enumeration.

Files: `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue`, `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (slim down).

### P20 — Polish round 2 — 1–2 commits

- `chore(p20-polish): add @nuxtjs/og-image + OG image templates for root and extensions/[slug]`
- `test(p20-polish): playwright browse + detail + navigation specs`

Files: `nuxt.config.ts` (register module), `app/og/**` (per `@nuxtjs/og-image` convention), `tests/e2e/{browse,detail,navigation}.spec.ts`.

## Critical files (modified or created)

- New top-level: `cli/**` (P14), `components.json` (P15).
- New components: `app/components/ui/**` (P15), `app/components/filters/{DeptPicker,TagDrawer,CreatorPicker,PublisherPicker}.vue` (P16), `app/components/publish/**` (P17), `app/components/profile/**` (P18), `app/components/extension/{ExtHero,ExtAboutCard,ExtTabs,ExtRelatedList,InstallCommand,ShareButton,SaveButton}.vue` (P19).
- New pages: `app/pages/publish/[id]/edit.vue` (P17), `app/pages/profile.vue` (P18).
- New shared: `shared/validators/profile.ts` (P18).
- New server: `server/utils/queries/{facets,profile}.ts`, `server/api/internal/facets.get.ts`, `server/api/internal/profile/**`, `server/api/internal/publish/get-draft.get.ts` (P16/P17/P18).
- Rewritten: `app/pages/publish/new.vue` (P17), `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (P19), `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` (P16).
- Config: `nuxt.config.ts` (P20).
- Docs: `docs/plan.md` (P14 + as needed), `CLAUDE.md` (P14).

## Reuse map (existing code to lean on, not duplicate)

- `shared/validators/manifest.ts` — Zod schemas for wizard forms (P17).
- `shared/data/departments.ts`, `shared/tags.ts` — picker option sources (P16).
- `app/composables/useFilters.ts` — picker selection state (P16).
- `server/api/internal/publish/{create-draft,attach-file,submit,discard}.post.ts` — wizard backend (P17, already wired).
- `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts`, `extension-detail.ts`, `collections.ts` — profile section data sources (P18).
- `server/utils/auth.ts`, `app/composables/useAuth.ts` — session for profile + edit gates.
- Reka UI + shadcn-vue (in `package.json`) for all UI primitives (P15).
- `lib/db/queries/profile.ts`, `components/publish/wizard/**`, `components/profile/**` in `../agentcenter/` — design reference only; rewrite in Vue.

## Verification

- **Per-commit**: `bun run typecheck`.
- **Per-phase smoke**:
  - P14: `cli/dist/agentcenter login && agentcenter install <slug>` against a local Nitro server.
  - P15: render each primitive on a scratch route or via existing pages.
  - P16: open `/en/extensions`, drawer opens, dept/tag/creator/publisher pickers narrow results, URL query updates.
  - P17: sign in → `/en/publish/new` → 4 steps → preview updates live → submit → see entry on `/en/publish` → open `/en/publish/[id]/edit` → discard works.
  - P18: `/en/profile` loads, settings form submits, all 5 section tabs render real data.
  - P19: `/en/extensions/[slug]` shows hero + about side panel + 3 tabs + related list, install/share/save work.
  - P20: OG image renders for root and a detail page; Playwright specs pass.
- **Pre-PR**: `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + vitest + coverage). Coverage thresholds: `shared/**` ≥ 95% lines / 90% branches, `app/composables/**` ≥ 90%, `server/utils/**` ≥ 80%, `server/api/**` ≥ 70%, `app/components/**` ≥ 60% overall (≥ 80% for interactive).
- **Pre-PR**: Playwright e2e green locally.
- **Each PR description**: list the phases it covers; no special "deviation" note needed once P14a lands and CLAUDE.md is trimmed.
- **After each PR merges**: pull `main`, branch the next PR off it, pause for human go-ahead.
